content.full_text:
Christine S. Knight

City of Novato

May 21, 1990

Kathryn Donovan
Legal General Council
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, Cal 95814

Dear Miss Donovan:

On March 14, 1990 I spoke to one of your consultants with
FPPC about a reimbursement I would like to claim from my 1987
campaign account.

The consultant sent me a copy of the regulations, Title 2,
Division 6 of the Calif. Code of Regulation and advised me
to write directly to you for an opinion concerning my request.

During 1989, I accumulated auto mileage serving as mayor and
councilmember for Novato, with a total of 4,400 miles on my
personal car.

I would like to claim this mileage and receive monetary
reimbursement from my campaign account.

In reference to 18526.1 #3, it is obvious I've gone over the
45 days time limit since my mileage total of 4,400 plus miles
was accumulated during a one year tax period.

I have kept a daily log of this mileage for the record. I
did not turn in my totals every 45 days in 1989 because I
was under the impression I could deduct the mileage from my
income tax. Since than my accountant informs me otherwise.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

Christine S. Knight
Councilmember


310 Country Club Drive • Novato, California 94949 • (415) 382-1751


If there is text present in this document, it is too faint or unclear for me to transcribe accurately according to your requirement to copy text verbatim.

sections.question: I would like to claim this mileage and receive monetary reimbursement from my campaign account. In reference to 18526.1 +3, it is obvious I've gone over the 45 days time limit since my mileage total of 4,400 plus miles was accumulated during a one year tax Period.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a city councilmember/mayor claim reimbursement for 4,400 miles of personal vehicle mileage from her 1989 campaign account when the mileage was accumulated over a one-year period, exceeding the 45-day reporting requirement under California Political Practices Code Section 18526.1?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The writer references a prior March 14, 1990 consultation with an FPPC consultant and is now formally requesting an opinion. No formal CONCLUSION/SHORT ANSWER section exists. Some OCR errors present (e.g., 'Eair Potitical' for 'Fair Political', 'caI' for 'CA'). Document has handwritten stamp 'MAR 3{ 2 at 11:' visible at bottom.

Street Suite 800 Sacramento, Cal 95814 Dear Miss Donovan: On March 14, 1990 I spoke to one of your consultants with FPPC about a reimbursement I would like to claim from my 1987 campaign account. The consultant sent me a copy of the regulations, Title 2, Division 6 of the Calif. Code of Regulation and advised me to write directly to you for an opinion concerning my request. During 1989, I accumulated auto mileage serving as mayor and councilmember for Novato, with a total of 4,400 miles on my personal car. I would like to claim this mileage and receive monetary reimbursement from my campaign account. In reference to 18526.1 #3, it is obvious I've gone over the 45 days time limit since my mileage total of 4,400 plus miles was accumulated during a one year tax period. I have kept a daily log of this mileage for the record. I did not turn in my totals every 45 days in 1989 because I was under the impression I could deduct the mileage from my income tax. Since than my accountant informs me otherwise. Thank you for your consideration. Sincerely, Christine S. embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from Christine S. Knight, a Novato City Councilmember, requesting FPPC guidance on whether she can claim reimbursement for 4,400 miles of campaign-related mileage accumulated in 1989 from her campaign account, despite exceeding the 45-day reporting requirement.
